mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in CPIAUCSL signal accelerating inflation that erodes real corporate earnings and tightens financial conditions, causing negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when CPIAUCSL increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals a non-linear deterioration in labor demand that compresses household income growth and triggers a repricing of corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint elevation of consumer prices and the federal funds rate creates a financial conditions squeeze that simultaneously raises discount rates and input costs, compounding downward pressure on equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: In periods where both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS are above their respective 12-month rolling medians, S&P 500 20-day returns are positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of restrictive monetary policy, month-over-month declines in retail sales signal that higher interest rates are successfully choking off consumer demand, validating a hard-landing narrative and driving negative S&P 500 forward 5-day returns.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5% and RSAFS declines month-over-month, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month contractions in industrial production signal a negative shock to productive capacity and inventory accumulation that precedes earnings guidance cuts, leading to negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when INDPRO declines.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts below 1,500 thousand units signals a contraction in residential investment and construction employment that spills over into broader credit-sensitive sectors, generating negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of periods when HOUST is below 1,500 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment coinciding with elevated policy rates creates a dual drag on corporate margins from falling revenues and higher interest expense, accelerating equity drawdowns and producing negative S&P 500 forward 5-day returns.
failure_condition: When UNRATE and FEDFUNDS both rise month-over-month, S&P 500 5-day returns are positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals expanding aggregate demand that supports top-line revenue growth and operating leverage, driving positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters when GDPC1 grows.
